popup_text: This design choice enables RLMs to scale beyond the limitations of traditional LLMs' context windows, allowing for more efficient and effective long-context processing.
highlight_text: treats long prompts as part of an external environment
annotation_text: This design choice enables RLMs to scale beyond the limitations of traditional LLMs' context windows, allowing for more efficient and effective long-context processing.
narration_text: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) treat long prompts as part of an external environment and allow the LLM to programmatically examine, decompose, and recursively call itself over snippets of the prompt.

popup_text: This design choice enables RLMs to tackle limitations of expressive power in recent work on coding agents, retrieval agents, and sub-agent delegation.
annotation_text: This design choice enables RLMs to tackle limitations of expressive power in recent work on coding agents, retrieval agents, and sub-agent delegation.
narration_text: RLMs make three simple design choices that are missing from existing scaffolds: symbolic recursion, a symbolic handle to the user prompt, and a separate function for invoking a sub-RLM.

popup_text: This design choice enables RLMs to handle long prompts and outputs without being limited by the context window of the underlying LLM, resulting in improved performance and scalability.
annotation_text: This design choice enables RLMs to handle long prompts and outputs without being limited by the context window of the underlying LLM, resulting in improved performance and scalability.
narration_text: RLMs achieve unbounded input and output tokens and an unbounded semantic horizon, enabling them to perform Ω(|P|) or Ω(|P|2) semantic work.

popup_text: This result highlights the effectiveness of RLMs in overcoming limitations of traditional LLMs and context compaction methods, enabling more efficient and effective long-context processing.
highlight_text: substantially better performance at long-context processing
annotation_text: This result highlights the effectiveness of RLMs in overcoming limitations of traditional LLMs and context compaction methods, enabling more efficient and effective long-context processing.
narration_text: RLMs outperform direct LLM calls and context compaction, with substantially better performance at long-context processing, often by double-digit percentage gains.

popup_text: This result suggests that RLMs are a flexible and adaptable architecture that can be fine-tuned for specific tasks and domains, enabling more efficient and effective deployment.
highlight_text: can be improved quickly with little additional training
annotation_text: This result suggests that RLMs are a flexible and adaptable architecture that can be fine-tuned for specific tasks and domains, enabling more efficient and effective deployment.
narration_text: RLMs can be improved quickly with little additional training, demonstrating that they can be fine-tuned for specific tasks and domains.

popup_text: This result highlights the cost-effectiveness of RLMs, enabling developers to achieve improved performance and scalability at a comparable cost to traditional LLMs.
highlight_text: comparable cost to direct LLM calls
annotation_text: This result highlights the cost-effectiveness of RLMs, enabling developers to achieve improved performance and scalability at a comparable cost to traditional LLMs.
narration_text: RLMs have comparable cost to direct LLM calls, but with improved performance and scalability, making them a more efficient and effective choice for long-context processing.
